mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) have been in a multi-quarter downtrend from cycle peaks, signaling contracting residential investment. Continued declines in HOUST precede weaker consumer wealth effects and construction employment, dragging on S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1427k units, down sharply from 2005-06 peaks above 2000k
failure_condition: Rank IC between HOUST monthly change and forward 20d SPX return stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year walk-forward window, or SPX posts positive returns in >55% of months with declining HOUST.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) of more than 0.3 percentage points over a rolling 3-month window signals labor market deterioration consistent with an emerging recession, which historically precedes negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter as earnings expectations are revised down.
failure_condition: In triggered episodes, SPX 60-day forward returns are positive more than 50% of the time, or the effect size is not statistically distinguishable from zero (p > 0.10) across historical triggers pre-2008.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary easing cycle, sequential reductions in FEDFUNDS lower the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows and ease financial conditions, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month. This mechanism is conditional on the economy already being in an identified easing regime rather than a hiking cycle.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS recorded at 4.1 in prior month snapshot
failure_condition: SPX forward 20d returns during periods of declining FEDFUNDS are not significantly higher than during flat/rising FEDFUNDS periods (t-test p > 0.05), or rank IC < 0.03 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) indicates a broad-based consumer demand slowdown that is more predictive of equity weakness than either series alone, because it captures both durable-goods investment and discretionary spending retrenchment jointly. Joint decline over 3 months predicts negative SPX returns over the following quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1427k, RSAFS at 689443M as of snapshot
failure_condition: Joint-decline signal produces forward 60d SPX returns not statistically different from the unconditional baseline (p > 0.10), or the interaction term adds no incremental rank IC versus either single series.

mechanism_narrative: A decelerating or declining trend in Industrial Production (INDPRO) reflects weakening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which leads earnings downgrades for cyclical sectors and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 104.5175 as of two months prior
failure_condition: SPX 20d forward returns show no significant negative correlation with INDPRO 3-month trend (rank IC < 0.03) across rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When headline CPI (CPIAUCSL) year-over-year inflation crosses above a level consistent with elevated inflation expectations, markets price in higher probability of continued or renewed monetary tightening, compressing valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL index at 298.112 one month prior to decision date
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns following CPI YoY prints above 4% are not significantly negative (p > 0.10) across historical triggered episodes, or reverse in sign in more than 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A stagflation regime, defined jointly by elevated FEDFUNDS combined with rising CPIAUCSL, compresses both corporate margins (via input costs) and equity valuations (via discount rates) simultaneously, producing outsized negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter beyond what either series predicts in isolation.
failure_condition: Joint-trigger episodes fail to show 60d forward SPX returns significantly more negative than single-condition episodes (p > 0.10), indicating no incremental interaction effect.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month declines in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signal immediate labor market weakening that investors react to quickly, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next trading week as risk sentiment deteriorates on payroll release days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 153743 thousand persons as of one month prior
failure_condition: Rank IC between monthly PAYEMS change and forward 5d SPX return remains below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or the sign of the relationship flips in more than 40% of rolling windows.
